qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Numeric range expansion generates all elements before max limit check, enabling DoS
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Matches the advisory's @isaacs/brace-expansion numeric brace expansion root cause: attacker-controlled numeric ranges reach expand(), which eagerly generates an unbounded result set before an effective limit prevents resource exhaustion.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded recursion in expand_ and parseCommaParts causes stack overflow
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause from the advisory: this finding concerns recursion depth and stack overflow on deeply nested braces, not eager unbounded expansion of repeated numeric brace ranges.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Zero increment in numeric sequence causes infinite loop and denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause from the advisory: this finding depends on an increment value of zero causing a non-advancing loop, not repeated numeric ranges causing exponential eager expansion.

selected_finding.title: Numeric range expansion generates all elements before max limit check, enabling DoS

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding identifies the same unbounded eager numeric brace expansion behavior described by GHSA-7h2j-956f-4vf2. The copied PoC invoked @isaacs/brace-expansion@5.0.0 with the advisory payload {0..99}{0..99}{0..99}{0..99}{0..99}; the call entered expand() and the Node.js child process aborted with JavaScript heap out of memory before returning.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-7h2j-956f-4vf2 describes denial of service from repeated numeric brace ranges that synchronously generate 100^5 combinations.
numeric_range_dos_bypass/vulnerability_report.json identifies src/index.ts:183-206 numeric sequence generation and a late max check at src/index.ts:216 as the resource-exhaustion root cause.
.judge/poc_runs/numeric_range_dos_bypass/run.log shows a sanity expansion returned length=1000, then the exact advisory payload entered @isaacs/brace-expansion@5.0.0 expand().
.judge/poc_runs/numeric_range_dos_bypass/run.log shows FATAL ERROR: Reached heap limit Allocation failed - JavaScript heap out of memory, with no return marker from the advisory payload expansion.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC installed @isaacs/brace-expansion@5.0.0, verified a smaller repeated range returned, then called expand() with {0..99}{0..99}{0..99}{0..99}{0..99}. The child process printed the pre-expand marker and aborted with JavaScript heap out of memory before any return marker.
